reviews.text: I had a wonderful time here listening to live tango music and watching people dancing. Super atmospheric place where a lot of locals come to enjoy the ambiance. Very nice bar staff; I had some lovely chats wish people. Nice little patio inside also for cooling down a little. Glass of red wine 🍷 was 5000 ars and it was very tasty. I understand that there are live music shows every night. I will definitely be back during my next visit to Buenos Aires. 👌👌👌👌👌

This is where the locals and tourists enjoy a drink at night and listen to live music.  The live music is free. You tip the musicians whatever you think they are worth.  Look at the man in the street, standing next to the window.  He did not move a hair for the entire song...

A true gem of a bar with rich history, providing a stage for local Argentinian artists to share their passion for music with an engaged audience.

While visiting Buenos Aires, we discovered this one-of-a-kind treasure. It radiates authenticity and passion, with unforgettable tango nights, excellent prices, and outstanding service.

There’s no cover charge for the live performances—you simply tip the artists as much as you wish, which adds to the charm and accessibility of the experience.

This place is an absolute jewel, filled with beautiful energy. I highly recommend it to anyone who wants to immerse themselves in the vibrant local art scene.

I think I am in love with this place. If you are lucky you might get to have a seat like me close to the music and have 4 magical hours here. Please bring cash to tip the musicians or get them a drink like I did (cause I ran out of cash for the second act). Magical place, magical tango music. Thank you

Best place to feel real tango song of old Argenina
Take some tips with you please😉 entrance free and it is worth to pay tips for great live songs. Most great evening here if you wanna feel native argentinos vibes #ionkinberg_travel
